# DCP — Datacenter Compute Platform

> DCP is the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's sovereign AI compute platform. It exposes an OpenAI-compatible inference API, on-demand whole-GPU rental ("pods"), persistent storage volumes, and an autonomous agent layer — all served from Saudi-owned hardware inside the Kingdom (full PDPL / data-residency compliance). DCP is built to be used by AI agents and software, not only humans: the inference endpoint is a drop-in replacement for `api.openai.com`, and an official MCP server lets any agent rent GPUs, run inference, and manage workloads through tool calls.

## For agents: start here

- **Agent guide (start here):** [/agents](https://dcp.sa/agents) — the zero-human flow, the MCP catalog + config, the GPU price table, and the machine-readable money signals, written for both humans and agents.
- **Product map:** [/pods](https://dcp.sa/pods), [/inference](https://dcp.sa/inference), [/fine-tuning](https://dcp.sa/fine-tuning), [/batch](https://dcp.sa/batch), [/dedicated-deployments](https://dcp.sa/dedicated-deployments), [/benchmarks](https://dcp.sa/benchmarks), [/models/allam](https://dcp.sa/models/allam), [/models/qwen-arabic](https://dcp.sa/models/qwen-arabic), and [/pricing](https://dcp.sa/pricing).
- **Renter dashboard readiness:** Authenticated `/renter/dashboard` summarizes Inference, Prompt cache, Batch, LoRA/adapters, and Pods readiness from `/v1/models`, `/v1/prompt-cache/settlement/readiness`, `/api/batches/readiness`, `/api/lora/readiness`, and current pod runway state. The dashboard is read-only: it does not launch pods, dispatch inference, route adapter traffic, run training, enable discounts, mutate billing, settle providers, or change balances.
- **Zero-human onboarding:** `POST https://api.dcp.sa/api/renters/agent-register` (no auth needed; optional JSON body `{email,label,use_case}`) → `201 { "api_key": "dcp-renter-…", "trial_credit_sar": 20, "balance_sar": 20 }`. A real, immediately-usable key + a 20 SAR trial credit, no human and no email click. (The full 100 SAR trial is the human email-verified signup.)
- **Inference (OpenAI-compatible):** Base URL `https://api.dcp.sa/v1`. Use any OpenAI SDK by setting `base_url` to it and `Authorization: Bearer <renter_api_key>` (also accepts `x-renter-key`). Endpoints: `POST /v1/chat/completions`, `GET /v1/models` (each entry has an `available` flag). Both `dcp-renter-` and `dc1-sk-` key prefixes work.
- **Model pages:** `/models/allam` and `/models/qwen-arabic` are public, catalog-aware product pages. They read `GET /v1/models` for provider-backed availability and `GET /api/models/benchmarks/readiness` for Arabic-quality claim guards. ALLaM may be catalog-only when `provider_count=0`; Qwen-family rows are serveable only when `/v1/models` says `provider_count>0`.
- **MCP server:** [dcp-mcp](https://github.com/dhnpmp-tech/dcp-mcp) — install with `npx -y github:dhnpmp-tech/dcp-mcp` (the npm package `@dcp/mcp` is coming soon). Eleven tools: `register_agent`, `list_models`, `chat`, `get_balance`, `list_gpus`, `create_pod`, `get_pod`, `extend_pod`, `stop_pod`, `rent_volume`, `get_volume`. `register_agent` is unauthenticated (it mints the first key); the rest take a renter key. The native way for an MCP-capable agent (Claude, etc.) to use DCP.
- **Safe money:** money routes (`POST /api/pods`, `POST /api/pods/{id}/extend`, `POST /api/volumes/rent`) accept an `Idempotency-Key` header (a retry with the same key returns the SAME pod/charge), and return a machine-readable `HTTP 402 { code:"insufficient_balance", required_sar, balance_sar, currency:"SAR", topup_url, retryable:true }` (creating no pod) when the wallet is short.
- **Minimum-balance policy:** `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/renters/me/minimum-balances` (Bearer billing/admin renter key) returns a read-only contract for v1 estimate preflight, on-demand paid-credit gates, volume quotes, blocked future billing rails, `credit_policy` separation between grant/trial credit and paid credit, and `trial_classification` derived from credit provenance. It does not create payments, launch pods, dispatch inference, enable discounts, change trial accounting, change account classification, change paid-credit policy, or change 402 enforcement.
- **Pod trial-routing policy:** `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/pods/trial-routing/readiness` returns the read-only contract for current trial-credit routing: trial status is derived from `renters.trial_grant_halala`, trial credit covers DCP/community capacity, high-demand capacity requires paid credit, and renter-facing surfaces do not expose provider id, vendor, or internal supply-tier labels. The local proof command is `npm run proof:pod-trial-routing-readiness`.
- **Renter Pods workspace UX:** `/renter/pods` keeps Stage 1 workspace files collapsed for large manifests, includes folder/file search plus a summary -> one-folder -> Stage 2 path in the collapsed folder preview, and makes Stage 2 the actual launch GPU source of truth. The collapsed Stage 1 outline shows the folder count, busiest-folder drilldown, and direct Stage 2 handoff. A compact workspace decision map now summarizes Stage 1 files, Stage 2 GPU, and Stage 3 launch before the detailed file manager, including one-click busiest-folder drilldown, suggested GPU, exact `gpu_type` payload, and trial route. Stage 2 also shows a suggested GPU for the current template/workload/browse context while keeping the actual `gpu_type` request beside it. The launch command center answers trial handling before launch: no separate trial-account tag is live under grant-credit provenance, trial credit routes to DCP/community GPUs, high-demand GPUs require paid credit, and the final GPU source is either Auto-pick or a selected card. Stage 3 repeats the final Auto-pick/fixed-GPU `gpu_type` payload, workspace attachment, trial route, high-demand paid-credit rule, runtime, and quote state directly above the launch button. VRAM chips, workload guide, search, and sort are browse-only until a GPU card is selected; memory chips are browse controls, not a launch slider.
- **LoRA pod image proof:** `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/pods/images/readiness` defines the provider-host gate for the fat `dcp-compute:lora` pod image. Accepted evidence requires a `dcp.lora_pod_image_proof.v1` report with `verdict=PASS`, `generated_at`, `acceptance_gate=lora_pod_image_provider_host`, and `require_gpu=1`; CPU/local script checks are labeled `DRY_RUN` and do not prove GPU-ready LoRA pods.
- **Admin live acceptance gates:** `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/admin/live-acceptance-gates` (Admin token) returns the CI-safe `dcp.live_acceptance_gate_status.v1` packet with blocked live gates, latest evidence, claim guards, and per-gate `dcp.live_acceptance_operator_runbook.v1` runbooks. OpenAI-compatible streaming acceptance uses `DCP_OPENAI_SSE_PROOF_ALLOW_LIVE=1 npm run proof:openai-sse`; Anthropic-compatible streaming acceptance uses `DCP_ANTHROPIC_PROOF_ALLOW_LIVE=1 npm run proof:anthropic-sse`. The status packet does not run paid compute, mutate routing, create payments, change billing, change trial accounting, or unlock capability claims; local proof is `npm run proof:live-acceptance-status`.
- **Agent manifest:** [/.well-known/ai-plugin.json](https://dcp.sa/.well-known/ai-plugin.json)
- **OpenAPI spec:** [/docs/openapi.yaml](https://dcp.sa/docs/openapi.yaml)

## Inference

- Public page: https://dcp.sa/inference.
- Models available right now: query `GET https://api.dcp.sa/v1/models` (returns OpenAI-style `data[]` with an `available` flag — only `available: true` models are currently serveable). Catalog is Arabic-and-long-tail focused (Qwen family, ALLaM-class Arabic models).
- Model pricing contract: each `/v1/models.data[].pricing` includes `contract.version = dcp.model_token_pricing.v1`, `currency = SAR`, `billing_unit = per_1m_tokens`, `source_contract`, `usd_display_only = true`, and false claim guards for billing, settlement, provider selection, and request routing changes. SAR/halala fields are the pricing source of truth; USD fields are display only.
- Model capability contract: each `/v1/models.data[]` includes `capability_contract.version = dcp.model_capability_contract.v1`, `source = model_registry.use_cases`, live capability flags, gated product flags, and false claim guards. Treat prompt cache, Batch, LoRA, and Dedicated Deployment availability from `feature_readiness`, not from capability labels alone.
- Router policy discovery: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/v1/router/policies`. The public request-selectable policy today is the balanced default; cheapest, lowest-latency, Saudi-only, coding, and Arabic policies remain gated until route-specific proof exists. The response includes `proof_contract`, false `claim_guards`, per-policy `selection_guard`, and `proof_gates`; the local proof command is `npm run proof:router-policy-contract`.
- Advanced feature flags: `/v1/models` includes `feature_readiness` metadata for prompt cache, batch, LoRA, and dedicated deployment rails. These flags are evidence gates, not product-available promises.
- Prompt-cache live acceptance contract: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/v1/prompt-cache/readiness` exposes `dcp.prompt_cache_live_acceptance_evidence.v1` under `prompt_cache_provider_discount_smoke`. `DCP_PROMPT_CACHE_LIVE_PROOF_ALLOW=1 npm run proof:prompt-cache-live-settlement` can prove live hash-only miss/hit measurement and no-discount guards, but cached-input discounts, provider KV-cache control, discounted settlement, and pricing/model flags remain gated by future discount evidence.
- Auth: a renter API key (`Bearer` token). Get one at https://dcp.sa (renter signup) and fund the wallet (SAR, prepaid).
- Pricing: per-token, cost-plus, billed in SAR. See `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/pricing`.

## GPU compute (pods)

- Public page: https://dcp.sa/pods. The older `/containers` URL remains a compatibility URL.
- Rent a whole GPU with root + Jupyter + SSH. `POST https://api.dcp.sa/api/pods` `{ "duration_minutes": N }` (Bearer renter key). Returns a pod id; poll `GET /api/pods/{id}` for `access_url` (Jupyter), `ssh_command`, `ends_at`, `seconds_remaining`.
- Extend without restart: `POST /api/pods/{id}/extend` `{ "extend_minutes": N }`.
- Stop early (prorated refund): `DELETE /api/pods/{id}`.
- Billing is prepaid per GPU-second in SAR; unused time is refunded on stop.
- Trial-routing readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/pods/trial-routing/readiness` defines the current no-vendor-exposure policy for trial credit, paid credit, DCP/community capacity, and high-demand capacity before launch.

### GPU types + indicative price (per hour)

Whole, dedicated NVIDIA GPUs, in-Kingdom (PDPL-compliant). On-demand types are priced cost-plus from the live market, so each is a "from" floor (it floats with the upstream rate, refreshed about every 4 minutes); the native RTX 3090 is a Saudi-owned community card at its configured rate. USD ≈ at the SAMA peg (1 USD ≈ 3.75 SAR); billing is in SAR. Live availability per type: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/renters/available-providers`.

| GPU | VRAM | Type | from SAR/hr | ≈ USD/hr |
|-----|------|------|-------------|----------|
| NVIDIA H200 | 141 GB | on-demand | 23.05 | 6.15 |
| NVIDIA H100 | 80 GB | on-demand | 17.27 | 4.61 |
| NVIDIA A100 | 80 GB | on-demand | 7.30 | 1.95 |
| NVIDIA L40S | 48 GB | on-demand | 5.20 | 1.39 |
| NVIDIA RTX 5090 | 32 GB | on-demand | 5.20 | 1.39 |
| NVIDIA RTX 4090 | 24 GB | on-demand | 3.62 | 0.97 |
| NVIDIA RTX 3090 | 24 GB | native | 2.50 | 0.67 |

Apple Silicon (M2-class) is also live in-Kingdom for inference-only workloads.

## Fine-tuning and LoRA

- Public page: https://dcp.sa/fine-tuning.
- Current state: DCP exposes LoRA/Fine-Tuning readiness, dataset validation, training-job metadata, model-card stubs, adapter registry rows, and deployment intent records.
- Readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/lora/readiness` (Bearer renter key) returns current gates and claim guards.
- Tinker-style loop gates live under `.tinker_loop` in the same readiness packet. Create LoRA, forward/backward, optimizer step, save weights, sample, and evaluate are contract-only and disabled until GPU-host loop proof exists.
- Training jobs and adapters are metadata/proof contracts today. Public managed training, public adapter serving, Tinker compatibility, quality claims, low-level loop APIs, and adapter route traffic remain gated until GPU-host artifact proof and vLLM load proof exist.
- Adapter artifact policy: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/artifacts/readiness` and `npm run proof:adapter-artifact-policy` define renter/adapter-scoped `adapter.safetensors` and `model-card.json` keys plus checksum guards; artifact uploads, object-store writes, adapter serving, route traffic, billing, and Tinker compatibility remain gated.
- Adapter endpoint smoke readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/endpoints/smoke/readiness`, disabled renter-scoped `GET/POST /api/adapters/{adapter_id}/deployments/{deployment_id}/endpoint-smoke`, `npm run proof:adapter-endpoint-smoke`, `npm run proof:adapter-endpoint-smoke-status`, and `npm run proof:adapter-endpoint-smoke-submission` define strict load proof, no-record status, funded principal, deterministic request, response hash, latency, token totals, and adapter trace requirements while smoke recording, route traffic, usage writes, billing, invoices, payouts, raw prompts/responses, and Tinker compatibility remain gated.
- Adapter usage attribution readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/usage/attribution/readiness` and `npm run proof:adapter-usage-attribution` define deployment, adapter, endpoint, checksum, provider, request, scoped-key, token, cost, and pending-settlement fields while adapter usage writes, adapter billing, invoices, payouts, route changes, and Tinker compatibility remain gated.
- Adapter settlement readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/settlement/readiness` and `npm run proof:adapter-settlement-readiness` define provider/platform split, settlement-status, usage-attribution, minimum-balance, and founder-approval prerequisites while provider payouts, platform revenue splits, invoices, balance mutations, adapter billing, route changes, raw prompts/responses, and Tinker compatibility remain gated.
- Adapter billing approval readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/billing/approval/readiness` and `npm run proof:adapter-billing-approval` define evidence-packet hash, local-roadmap proof, production smoke, and founder signoff prerequisites while adapter billing, route traffic, usage writes, invoices, provider payouts, balance mutations, raw prompts/responses, and Tinker compatibility remain gated.
- Adapter billing readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/billing/readiness` and `npm run proof:adapter-billing-readiness` define strict load-proof, endpoint-smoke, funded-principal, minimum-balance, usage-attribution, settlement, and founder-approval prerequisites while adapter billing, usage writes, invoices, provider payouts, route changes, and Tinker compatibility remain gated.
- Adapter vLLM live acceptance contract: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/lora/readiness` exposes `dcp.adapter_vllm_live_acceptance_evidence.v1` under `adapter_deployments.live_acceptance.vllm_load_billing_smoke`; `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/billing/readiness` exposes the same evidence list under `policy.live_acceptance`; `DCP_ADAPTER_VLLM_LIVE_PROOF_ALLOW=1 npm run proof:adapter-vllm-live-load` must prove readiness claims, funded principal, adapter checksum, deployment intent, strict vLLM load proof, endpoint smoke, usage attribution, billing policy, and claim boundary before adapter serving, route traffic, endpoint smoke, billing, dedicated-deployment, or Tinker-adjacent claims can pass.
- Adapter deployment records: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/adapters/deployments` (Bearer renter key) lists renter deployment intents across adapters. Rows do not imply traffic unless the backend proof says `route_traffic: true`.

## Batch inference

- Public page: https://dcp.sa/batch.
- Current state: JSONL validation, batch metadata records, line-ledger rows, result-manifest proof shape, and readiness gates exist.
- Readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/batches/readiness` (Bearer renter key).
- Live acceptance contract: readiness exposes `dcp.batch_live_acceptance_evidence.v1` under `batch_live_execution_discount_smoke`; `DCP_BATCH_LIVE_PROOF_ALLOW=1 npm run proof:batch-live-execution` must prove readiness, batch create, completed polling, result manifest, checksum-verified download, per-line execution proof, discounted settlement, and model capability flag evidence before batch execution or discount claims can turn true.
- Create/list/read records: `POST /api/batches`, `GET /api/batches`, `GET /api/batches/{batch_id}`, `GET /api/batches/{batch_id}/lines`, `GET /api/batches/{batch_id}/results`.
- Important gate: production batch worker execution, completed-result downloads without proof, discounted settlement, and `/v1/models` batch product-available flags remain off until worker/result/billing proof is enabled.

## Dedicated deployments

- Public page: https://dcp.sa/dedicated-deployments.
- Current state: adapter deployment intent rows and serving-load-proof contracts exist. Dedicated endpoints are the intended bridge from LoRA adapters to billed inference.
- Traffic gate: route traffic remains disabled until serving load proof matches deployment id, adapter id, base model, mode, endpoint id, and artifact checksum.
- Product boundary: single-adapter live merge is the first target; multi-LoRA serving waits for controlled vLLM smoke proof before it becomes a public claim.

## Benchmarks and evals

- Public page: https://dcp.sa/benchmarks.
- Current state: model benchmark metadata and provider benchmark contracts are visible, with public model metrics gated by live provider measurement and `measured_at` evidence.
- Readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/models/benchmarks/readiness`.
- Evaluator readiness: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/readiness`.
- Eval-job schema contract: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/jobs/schema` defines the renter-scoped eval job record, dataset checksum policy, metrics, result artifact manifest, and billing guards.
- Metadata eval jobs: renter-authenticated `POST/GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/jobs` and `GET /api/evals/jobs/{evalJobId}` create, list, and read draft metadata records only. Workers, result artifacts, billing, reports, rankings, and quality claims remain gated.
- Evaluator worker gate: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/worker/readiness` proves queue dispatch, worker execution, result writing, and billing hooks are disabled by default until worker/result/billing proof exists.
- Worker dry-run fixture: `npm run proof:evaluator-worker-dry-run-fixture` simulates a draft eval metadata job queue item and invokes the result-writer dry run, writing only temporary proof artifacts while job status, production artifacts, result endpoints, billing, reports, and rankings remain unchanged/off.
- Result manifest schema: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/results/schema` defines required checksums and publication guards for future eval artifacts; `GET /api/evals/jobs/{evalJobId}/results` remains non-live.
- Result artifact storage policy: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/results/artifacts/readiness` and `npm run proof:evaluator-artifact-storage-policy` define renter/job-scoped future result object keys and checksum guards; object-store writes, signed downloads, result endpoints, billing, reports, and rankings remain gated.
- Result access policy: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/results/access/readiness` and `npm run proof:evaluator-result-access-policy` define owner-match, result-available, artifact-policy, and checksum guards before result endpoints or signed downloads can go live.
- Disabled result endpoint: renter-authenticated `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/jobs/{evalJobId}/results` returns a disabled-result contract instead of artifacts; `npm run proof:evaluator-result-endpoint-disabled` proves no manifest, storage key, signed URL, billing, report, ranking, or quality claim is exposed.
- Signed download policy: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/results/downloads/readiness` and `npm run proof:evaluator-signed-download-policy` define result-access, artifact, checksum, content-type, and 60-900 second expiry requirements; signed URLs, object-store buckets, storage keys, result endpoints, billing, reports, and rankings remain gated.
- Result writer dry-run: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/evals/results/writer/readiness` and `npm run proof:evaluator-result-writer-dry-run` prove a manifest can be written to temporary proof storage only; production artifact writes and downloads remain gated.
- Benchmark feed: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/models/benchmarks`.
- Important gate: public Arabic-quality claims, customer case studies, frontier-model comparisons, and model rankings remain off until DCP has an approved dataset, fixed eval harness, artifact checksum, model/provider metadata, and baseline policy approval.

## Persistent storage (volumes)

- Rent an exclusive, in-Kingdom persistent volume: `POST https://api.dcp.sa/api/volumes/rent` `{ "size_gb": 10|20|30 }`. A pod's `/workspace` then persists across pods and providers.
- `GET /api/volumes/me` (status + usage + pool), `DELETE /api/volumes` (release).

## Wallet & account

- Balance: `GET https://api.dcp.sa/api/renters/balance` (Bearer). Top up in SAR via the wallet at https://dcp.sa.

## What DCP is (positioning)

- Sovereign, in-Kingdom AI compute: data, models, storage, and control plane inside Saudi Arabia, under Saudi law — a structural advantage no foreign API can match.
- A two-sided marketplace of Saudi-owned GPUs (RTX-class) and Apple-Silicon Macs, unified by a self-healing mesh and a smart routing layer that presents one clean OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- Not a token pipe: you can run inference, fine-tune and host your own models, rent whole GPUs, attach persistent storage, and deploy agents — all in one place.

## Use cases (what people ask for, and what DCP answers)

- "Rent an H200 / H100 / A100 / L40S / RTX 5090 / RTX 4090 on demand" → whole-GPU pods, per-second billing in SAR, root + Jupyter + SSH in ~1 minute. See GPU table above.
- "OpenAI-compatible inference API alternative" → drop-in `base_url = https://api.dcp.sa/v1` with an OpenAI SDK and a DCP renter key. No rewrite.
- "Batch inference" → build against the JSONL validation and metadata/line-ledger APIs now; do not claim execution discounts until batch worker and settlement proof are live.
- "LoRA fine-tuning / Tinker-like workflow" → DCP has readiness, training metadata, adapter registry, adapter artifact policy, endpoint-smoke policy/status/submission contracts, usage-attribution policy, settlement policy, billing-approval policy, deployment-intent contracts, and disabled Tinker-style loop gates; inspect `/api/adapters/artifacts/readiness`, `/api/adapters/endpoints/smoke/readiness`, disabled `GET/POST /api/adapters/{adapter_id}/deployments/{deployment_id}/endpoint-smoke`, `/api/adapters/usage/attribution/readiness`, `/api/adapters/settlement/readiness`, `/api/adapters/billing/approval/readiness`, `npm run proof:adapter-artifact-policy`, `npm run proof:adapter-endpoint-smoke`, `npm run proof:adapter-endpoint-smoke-status`, `npm run proof:adapter-endpoint-smoke-submission`, `npm run proof:adapter-settlement-readiness`, and `npm run proof:adapter-billing-approval`; public managed training, Tinker compatibility, low-level loop APIs, and adapter serving are gated by GPU artifact proof, vLLM load proof, deterministic endpoint smoke, and usage/money-policy proof.
- "Dedicated LoRA endpoint" → use deployment-intent records, load-proof gates, endpoint-smoke readiness, adapter usage attribution readiness, and adapter billing readiness; traffic routes only after proof matches deployment id, adapter id, base model, mode, endpoint id, and artifact checksum, and billing waits for deterministic endpoint smoke with response hash, latency, token totals, adapter trace, usage attribution, and money-policy approval.
- "Arabic AI benchmark / eval results" → inspect `/benchmarks`, `/api/models/benchmarks/readiness`, `/api/evals/jobs/schema`, `/api/evals/worker/readiness`, `/api/evals/results/schema`, `/api/evals/results/artifacts/readiness`, `/api/evals/results/access/readiness`, `/api/evals/results/downloads/readiness`, `/api/evals/results/writer/readiness`, `npm run proof:evaluator-worker-dry-run-fixture`, `npm run proof:evaluator-artifact-storage-policy`, `npm run proof:evaluator-result-access-policy`, `npm run proof:evaluator-result-endpoint-disabled`, `npm run proof:evaluator-signed-download-policy`, and renter metadata jobs at `/api/evals/jobs`; public quality comparisons stay gated until reproducible eval artifacts exist.
- "MCP server to rent a GPU / run inference" → the official DCP MCP server (`npx -y github:dhnpmp-tech/dcp-mcp`) exposes self-registration (`register_agent`), inference, GPU-type listing (`list_gpus`), pods, volumes, and balance as tools for MCP-capable agents (Claude, etc.).
- "AI agent that buys its own compute with no human" → `register_agent` (or `POST /api/renters/agent-register`) mints a real key + 20 SAR trial with no human; idempotent money routes + the HTTP 402 funding signal let the agent rent and pay safely. See https://dcp.sa/agents.
- "Minimum balance / paid credit needed before compute" → inspect `/api/renters/me/minimum-balances` and `/api/pods/trial-routing/readiness`; trial account status is a derived credit-provenance state, on-demand/high-demand GPUs require paid available credit for the quoted pod cost, trial credit covers DCP/community capacity, while v1 inference uses per-request estimate preflight and future LoRA/batch/eval billing remains blocked until proof exists.
- "Sovereign / in-Kingdom AI compute in Saudi Arabia / the Gulf" → data, models, storage, and control plane all inside KSA under PDPL; cross-border frontier models only by explicit per-tenant opt-in.
- "Fine-tune & host your own model" → rent a whole GPU and attach a persistent in-Kingdom volume today; managed LoRA training and adapter serving progress through the proof gates above.

## How DCP compares (truthful framing)

- vs US-based GPU clouds (RunPod / Vast / Lambda): DCP keeps data, hardware, and settlement inside Saudi Arabia under Saudi law (PDPL), billed in SAR — a data-residency guarantee a foreign API cannot match for KSA/Gulf workloads.
- vs hosted inference APIs (Together / DeepInfra / Modal / Replicate): DCP's `/v1` is OpenAI-compatible (drop-in base_url) and runs in-Kingdom; it also offers whole-GPU pods and an agent-native MCP server, not only a token pipe.
- Honest catalog: only models with `available: true` from `GET /v1/models` are serveable right now; capacity is published only after live provider verification, never simulated.

## Docs

- Agent guide (zero-human onboarding, MCP, money signals): https://dcp.sa/agents
- Human docs: https://dcp.sa/docs
- Inference: https://dcp.sa/inference
- ALLaM model page: https://dcp.sa/models/allam
- Qwen Arabic model page: https://dcp.sa/models/qwen-arabic
- GPU Pods: https://dcp.sa/pods
- Fine-Tuning: https://dcp.sa/fine-tuning
- Batch: https://dcp.sa/batch
- Dedicated Deployments: https://dcp.sa/dedicated-deployments
- Benchmarks: https://dcp.sa/benchmarks
- Pricing (GPU/hr + per-token, in SAR): https://dcp.sa/pricing
- This file: https://dcp.sa/llms.txt
